div.p: T=0: Client sends "withdraw $100" T=1: Server commits, applies T=2: Response LOST T=3: Client retries T=4: $200 withdrawn!
Why this works: Session state is part of the replicated state machine. When leader crashes:

ul.li: New leader has same session state

div.p: If sessions are lost after snapshot:

g.text: T=0 Client sends (clientId=abc, seq=5, cmd=PUT x=1)
T=1 Leader receives, appends to log at index 42
T=2 Leader replicates entry 42 to majority
T=3 Entry 42 committed, applied to state machine
T=4 Leader crashes BEFORE sending response!
T=5 New leader elected (has entry 42 replicated)
T=6 Client times out, retries (clientId=abc, seq=5, cmd=PUT x=1)
T=7 New leader's state machine checks:
T=8 New leader returns cached response {OK}

text.tspan: T=0 Client sends (clientId=abc, seq=5, cmd=PUT x=1)
T=1 Leader receives, appends to log at index 42
T=2 Leader replicates entry 42 to majority
T=3 Entry 42 committed, applied to state machine
T=4 Leader crashes BEFORE sending response!
T=5 New leader elected (has entry 42 replicated)
T=6 Client times out, retries (clientId=abc, seq=5, cmd=PUT x=1)
T=7 New leader's state machine checks:
T=8 New leader returns cached response {OK}

div.p: seq=5 <= lastSeq=5? YES → Return cached response Command NOT re-executed!
seq=5 > lastSeq=4? YES → Execute PUT x=1 Update: lastSeq=5 Cache: lastResp={OK}
Same pattern: client-provided unique key for deduplication
Consumer tracks offset per partition to avoid reprocessing
Server tracks request IDs to deduplicate retries
